title: crepis foetida f. glandulosa
intro: Crepis foetida f. glandulosa, also known as the hairy hawk's beard or stinking hawk's beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, and has been used to treat various ailments. Crepis foetida f. glandulosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it accessible for potential health benefits.
